evals.prompt: Add ID Capture to this blank UIViewController to scan passports, driver's licenses, and ID cards from any region. I need data from both sides of the document. When a document is recognized, show the full name, date of birth, and document number in an alert. When a document is rejected, show an appropriate message.
evals.expected_output: A complete UIViewController implementing IdCaptureListener with addListener called once at setup, camera lifecycle in viewWillAppear/viewDidDisappear, IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments for Passport, DriverLicense and IdCard, a FullDocumentScanner (required for both-sides capture), and alert-based result display.

assertions.text: Imports ScanditIdCapture (and optionally ScanditCaptureCore)
Calls DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey:) before using DataCaptureContext.shared
Uses Camera.default and calls context.setFrameSource
Applies IdCapture.recommendedCameraSettings to the camera
Creates DataCaptureView(context:frame:) and adds it as a subview
Creates IdCaptureSettings() and sets acceptedDocuments to include Passport(region: .any), DriverLicense(region: .any), and IdCard(region: .any)

Sets settings.scanner = IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocument: FullDocumentScanner()) — uses FullDocumentScanner (both sides required), does NOT use SingleSideScanner, settings.supportedDocuments, or settings.scannerType
Creates IdCapture(context: context, settings: settings) — does NOT use IdCapture.Create or IdCapture.forDataCaptureContext
Creates IdCaptureOverlay(idCapture:view:)
Implements idCapture(_:didCapture:capturedId:) — does NOT use didCaptureIn session:frameData:
Implements idCapture(_:didReject:reason:) — does NOT use didRejectIn session:frameData:
Sets idCapture.isEnabled = false inside didCapture before showing the alert
Re-enables idCapture.isEnabled = true when the alert is dismissed
Dispatches UI work to DispatchQueue.main or the main thread
Calls idCapture.addListener(self) once at setup time (e.g. in setupRecognition or viewDidLoad), NOT in viewWillAppear
Turns camera on in viewWillAppear and off in viewDidDisappear
Reads capturedId.fullName, capturedId.dateOfBirth, and capturedId.documentNumber from the CapturedId
Does NOT reference IdCaptureSession or FrameData
Does NOT reference AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier

evals.prompt: Update the ID Capture integration to reject expired IDs and holders under 21 years old. Show a distinct message for each rejection reason: expired document, underage holder, timeout, and any other rejection.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings updated with rejectExpiredIds = true and rejectHolderBelowAge = 21, and didReject callback updated with a switch on RejectionReason showing distinct messages for .documentExpired, .holderUnderage, .timeout, and a default case.

assertions.text: Sets settings.rejectExpiredIds = true
Sets settings.rejectHolderBelowAge = 21
Uses a switch statement on the RejectionReason parameter in didReject
Has a case for .documentExpired with a distinct user-facing message about the document being expired
Has a case for .holderUnderage with a distinct user-facing message about the age requirement
Has a case for .timeout with a distinct user-facing message about the capture timing out
Has a default case for all other rejection reasons
Does NOT use rejectHolderBelowAge as a String — it must be an Int literal (21)

evals.prompt: Configure ID Capture to accept ID cards and driver's licenses from the US, but explicitly reject passports.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments containing IdCard(region: .us) and DriverLicense(region: .us), and rejectedDocuments containing Passport(region: .any).

assertions.text: Sets acceptedDocuments to [IdCard(region: .us), DriverLicense(region: .us)] — does NOT put passports in acceptedDocuments
Sets rejectedDocuments to contain Passport — does NOT use acceptedDocuments to exclude passports
Does NOT confuse acceptedDocuments and rejectedDocuments — wanted documents go in acceptedDocuments, unwanted in rejectedDocuments

evals.prompt: I only need to read the barcode from US driver's licenses — I don't need the VIZ or MRZ.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments containing DriverLicense(region: .us) and scanner set to IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocument: SingleSideScanner(enablingBarcode: true, machineReadableZone: false, visualInspectionZone: false)).

assertions.text: Sets acceptedDocuments to [DriverLicense(region: .us)]
Uses SingleSideScanner with enablingBarcode: true and machineReadableZone: false and visualInspectionZone: false — does NOT use FullDocumentScanner
Wraps the scanner in IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocument: SingleSideScanner(...))

evals.prompt: I only need to read the MRZ from passports — I don't need the VIZ or barcode.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments containing Passport and scanner set to IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocument: SingleSideScanner(enablingBarcode: false, machineReadableZone: true, visualInspectionZone: false)).

assertions.text: Sets acceptedDocuments to include Passport
Uses SingleSideScanner with machineReadableZone: true and visualInspectionZone: false and enablingBarcode: false — does NOT use FullDocumentScanner
Wraps the scanner in IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocument: SingleSideScanner(...))

evals.prompt: Configure ID Capture to scan US driver's licenses only and verify them for forged AAMVA barcodes and data inconsistency between the front and back of the document. Also reject expired IDs. On rejection for inconsistent data, extract and display the front review image from the verification result. On a successful scan, also check for and display the review image if available.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings targeting DriverLicense(region: .us) with rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes, rejectInconsistentData, and rejectExpiredIds flags, setIncludeImage for croppedDocument, and correct VerificationResult access in both callbacks.

assertions.text: Sets acceptedDocuments to [DriverLicense(region: .us)] only
Sets settings.rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes = true
Sets settings.rejectInconsistentData = true
Sets settings.rejectExpiredIds = true
Calls settings.setIncludeImage(true, for: .croppedDocument) — required for frontReviewImage to be populated
Does NOT use AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier — verification is settings-driven
In didReject, accesses capturedId?.verificationResult.dataConsistency?.frontReviewImage when reason == .inconsistentData
Handles .inconsistentData, .forgedAamvaBarcode, and .documentExpired in didReject with distinct messages
Re-enables idCapture.isEnabled = true after showing the verification result

evals.prompt: The app already uses BarcodeCapture for scanning barcodes. Now add an ID scanning screen using IdCapture. Make sure the two modes don't interfere — the ID scanning screen should handle mode co-existence correctly.
evals.expected_output: Code that addresses BarcodeCapture/IdCapture co-existence — either by removing the existing mode before adding IdCapture (the typical approach for separate screens), or by explicitly acknowledging that simultaneous operation is intentional.

assertions.text: Addresses the BarcodeCapture/IdCapture co-existence concern — either calls context.removeCurrentMode() or context.removeAllModes() when switching modes, OR explicitly notes that simultaneous co-existence is intentional
Creates IdCapture(context: context, settings: settings)
Sets up IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments and scanner
Implements IdCaptureListener with didCapture and didReject
Manages camera lifecycle in viewWillAppear / viewDidDisappear
